figures.caption: TABLE I: Performance Comparison of FNN-PINN and SEA-PINN Models on PDE Cases
FIG. 5: Heat2D_ Multiscale(ID=7):Heatmaps of FNN-PINN and SEA-PINN at t=0.1s (Seed=1)

FIG. 6: Architecture of SEA-PINN within PINNs: The complete set of outputs from the nth hidden layer is taken as input to the weight generator, producing a weight vector. These weights modulate the hidden activations through element-wise multiplication, yielding weighted activations, which are then fully connected to the (n+ 1)th hidden layer.
